completed.title: Bot modular architecture — refactor monolithic index.ts into discord.js-standard module structure
Ingestion & entity extraction — proper descriptions, dedup, gpt-4.1-mini, scope tightening, cron fix
Graph & bot final polish — companion linking, entity extraction fix, Slop-first bot strategy, dashboard type breakdown
User persona system — bot learns per-user interaction style (tone, verbosity, format)
Member profiles — Discord avatars + profile card display in hub
Slop system message overhaul — kill soul file, per-member interaction preferences, 75% size reduction
UI polish & documentation refresh — cleaner design, light mode default, docs readability + content update
Event nodes — first-class events in sidebar, linked to recordings, Discord scheduling with double-booking prevention
Guides to skills migration — rename guides to skills, consolidate MCP guides, add read-only UI
Bot polish v2 — Slop-only, model upgrade to Sonnet 4.6, source linking
Visual pizazz — ASCII art header, Latent Space purple accent, terminal flair
Identity, dev process & repo cleanup — establish LSH as the product, set up workflow, fix stale code
Schema cleanup + typed entity model — finalize DB structure with node_type, port RA-H UX
Naming audit — align branding and naming to Latent Space Hub
Enable vector search — wire up Turso native vector_top_k, add FTS5, hybrid search
Content ingestion — backfill podcasts, articles, ainews, LatentSpaceTV (Jan 2025 → today)
Discord bots v1 — Sig & Slop, dual-personality bots backed by LS Wiki-Base
MCP server (NPX) + skills/guides — publishable package, shared interface for humans and bots
UX Overhaul — collapsible type navigation + simplified main workspace
Dashboard + Category Taxonomy — 8 canonical categories, dashboard overview, sidebar overhaul
Documentation overhaul v1 — initial full replacement of inherited RA-H docs
Auto-ingestion pipeline — hourly discovery, ingestion runs, and Discord fanout notifications
Hub node architecture — anchor LS properties and absorb storyline-ready narrative links
Discord bot v2 — grounded guides, temporal context, and deterministic channel triggers
Documentation overhaul v2 — user-facing /docs page, developer docs drift fixes, agent guides verification
Data refinement & hygiene — type migration, descriptions/notes, dates, edge cleanup

builders-club-prep.notes: Brad presenting LS Hub + Slop at Builders Club. Create presentation brief, demo flow script, pre-demo checklist, fallback screenshots. No code — documentation and preparation.

tasks.text: Part 1: Create presentation brief with talking points and structure
Part 2: Write demo flow script (8-step sequence)
Part 3: Pre-demo checklist — verify all surfaces working
Part 4: Capture fallback screenshots/recordings
Part 5: Quick polish pass on demo-visible surfaces

builders-club-prep.title: Builders Club presentation prep — talking points, demo flow, checklist, fallback assets

evals-dashboard.notes: Fix broken bot logging (column mismatch), capture tool calls + MCP operations + timing, build /evals dashboard to review traces, then extend with golden dataset (10-15 scenarios), automated runner, and scoring. Absorbed PRD-26 (eval suite). Uses existing chats table — no migration needed.

tasks.text: Part 1: Rewrite bot logging — align to chats schema, capture tool calls + timing
Part 2: Build /evals dashboard — list view + expanded trace view
Part 3: API route — paginated queries with filters
Part 4: Define eval types and scenario schema
Part 5: Create golden dataset — 10-15 scenarios
Part 6: Build eval runner + eval API endpoint
Part 7: Extend /evals dashboard — source filter, scenario results, aggregate metrics
Part 8: Add npm run evals script

evals-dashboard.title: Evals — interaction logging, trace dashboard, golden dataset & automated testing

inbox.notes: New /inbox route showing all Discord interactions grouped by user as conversations. Detail view per conversation. Inbox stats on dashboard. Reuses chats table. Future: email integration.

tasks.text: Part 1: API routes — /api/inbox (list conversations) + /api/inbox/[userId] (conversation detail)
Part 2: Build /inbox page — conversation list with user avatars, last message preview, unread count
Part 3: Conversation detail view — threaded message display with tool call traces
Part 4: Add inbox stats to dashboard (total conversations, active users, messages today)
Part 5: Add inbox to sidebar navigation

inbox.title: Inbox — conversation-style view of all bot interactions (Discord now, email later)

setup-handover.notes: No code. Owner does 3 tasks (invite bot, create webhooks, test channel), then Brad tests and switches to live.

tasks.text: Owner: Invite Slop bot to LS server
Owner: Create announcement webhook, send URL
Owner: Create yap webhook, send URL
Owner: Create #slop-testing channel + webhook
Brad: Test webhook post in #slop-testing
Brad: Test @Slop mention in #slop-testing
Brad: Test full ingestion pipeline
Brad: Switch to live channels + redeploy
Brad: Delete test channel, confirm first real ingestion

setup-handover.title: Setup & handover — get bot and webhooks live in the LS Discord server

ui-ux-cleanup.notes: Three issues: (1) ThreePanelLayout is a misnomer — only 2 panels, SplitHandle is dead code. Rename to AppLayout. (2) Focus/tab state from old multi-pane design lingers — audit, add back button, confirm cleanup on view switch. (3) Paper Club category doesn't show upcoming events because event_date DESC + limit=100 excludes future dates. Fix sort order to show upcoming first.

tasks.text: Step 1: Rename ThreePanelLayout to AppLayout, delete unused SplitHandle.tsx, update all imports
Step 2: Audit focus/tab state — add back button when node focused, verify cleanup on view switches
Step 3: Fix Paper Club upcoming events — change event_date sort to show upcoming first, verify client-side split

ui-ux-cleanup.title: Final UI/UX cleanup — layout naming, focus state audit, Paper Club upcoming events fix

skills-tools-audit.notes: Decoupled Slop from MCP. Direct Turso + local tools. Skills audited (6→4). Hub slop skills deleted. MCP is external-agents-only.

tasks.text: Phase 1: Replace McpGraphClient with direct Turso queries (src/db.ts + src/tools.ts)
Phase 2: Audit and refine skills (6→4)
Phase 3: Delete hub slop skills, update skillService/UI/docs
Cleanup: delete mcpGraphClient.ts, npm install in bots repo

skills-tools-audit.title: Decouple Slop from MCP + skills/tools audit

public-roadmap-contributing.notes: Expose the development process to non-technical community members. Public /docs/roadmap page (live from backlog.json) and /docs/contributing page (non-technical ways to help). No editing — read-only display surface.

tasks.text: Step 1: Roadmap API route — read backlog.json, return sanitized public summary
Step 2: Roadmap doc content — src/config/docs/roadmap.md with static framing
Step 3: RoadmapView component — cards for queue + list for recently shipped
Step 4: Contributing doc content — non-technical guide (suggest content, report issues, propose features, present at events)
Step 5: Add roadmap + contributing to docs navigation in docsService.ts
Step 6: Wire RoadmapView component into /docs/roadmap page

public-roadmap-contributing.title: Public roadmap & contributing guide — expose dev process for non-technical contributors

wiki-base-rebrand.notes: Rebrand to LS Wiki-Base. Terminology sweep, documentation unification and overhaul, code↔docs integration, database↔docs integration, living docs system, legibility pass. No schema migrations or UI changes — pure terminology + documentation.

tasks.text: Step 1: Terminology sweep — replace legacy terminology references with wiki-base
Step 2: Documentation unification — merge dev docs and user docs into one coherent tree
Step 3: Documentation overhaul — rewrite all docs for accuracy, completeness, legibility
Step 4: Code → docs integration — @docs JSDoc tags, source file references in docs
Step 5: Database → docs integration — auto-generate schema docs, drift detection
Step 6: Living docs system — pre-commit reminders, docs:check script, CLAUDE.md rules
Step 7: Legibility pass — consistent formatting, headers, updated dates, reading order

wiki-base-rebrand.title: LS Wiki-Base rebrand — terminology, documentation overhaul, code↔docs↔database integration

bot-write-guardrails.notes: Tighten DB write safety in latent-space-bots. Add a centralized write gate, explicit action policy checks, idempotency protections, and write audit logging so writes are intentional, explainable, and reviewable.

tasks.text: Step 1: Add write action/context types and action allowlist
Step 2: Implement writes/policy.ts with allow/block decisions and reasons
Step 3: Implement writes/guardrails.ts as single executeWrite wrapper
Step 4: Route mutating paths through guardrails (join, schedule, member updates, edge creation)
Step 5: Add idempotency and duplicate protections for join + event scheduling + edge creation
Step 6: Add write audit log sink (allowed/blocked/succeeded/failed)
Step 7: Add tests for policy decisions and idempotency; run build + manual Discord validation

bot-write-guardrails.title: Bot write guardrails — centralized policy, execution gate, idempotency, and audit logging
